column3: Classifier Lambda Unit — Valid Song Lyric
column4: Validate Classifier Lambda returns a valid mood label and confidence score for a song lyric payload
column5: anp-classifier-dev deployed; Bedrock model available in us-east-1; IAM role anp-classifier-lambda-role active
column6:
1. Invoke anp-classifier-dev directly via AWS CLI with payload {"text": "Amazing grace how sweet the sound", "content_type": "song"}
2. Parse response body
3. Verify mood_label is one of Joyful/Reflective/Peaceful/Uplifting/Worshipful/Hopeful
4. Verify confidence_score is a float between 0.0 and 1.0
column7: HTTP 200 with {"mood_label": "<valid label>", "confidence_score": <0.0-1.0>}
column14: Primary success path; mood label must be within defined vocabulary

column3: Classifier Lambda Unit — Valid Podcast Transcript
column4: Validate Classifier Lambda returns a valid mood label for a podcast transcript payload
column5: anp-classifier-dev deployed; Bedrock available; test transcript text available
column6:
1. Invoke anp-classifier-dev with {"text": "<faith-based podcast transcript excerpt>", "content_type": "podcast"}
2. Verify statusCode is 200
3. Verify mood_label in vocabulary
4. Verify confidence_score >= 0.0
column7: HTTP 200; mood_label in defined vocabulary; confidence_score in range
column14: Validates content_type=podcast path through Bedrock prompt

column3: Classifier Lambda Unit — Empty Text Field
column4: Validate Classifier Lambda returns HTTP 400 when the text field is empty
column5: anp-classifier-dev deployed; API Gateway request model validation active
column6: 1. Send POST /v1/classify with {"text": "", "content_type": "song"}\n2. Check response status code\n3. Verify error message is descriptive
column7: HTTP 400 with {"error": "ValidationError", "message": "text field is required"}
column14: API Gateway request model must reject before Lambda is invoked

column3: Classifier Lambda Unit — Missing API Key
column4: Validate API Gateway rejects POST /classify requests with no x-api-key header
column5: API Gateway anp-api-dev deployed; usage plan active
column6: 1. Send POST /v1/classify without x-api-key header\n2. Check response status code\n3. Verify request is logged in API Gateway access logs
column7: HTTP 403 Forbidden; request logged in /aws/apigateway/anp-api-access-logs
column14: Security control — must reject before any Lambda invocation

column3: Classifier Lambda Unit — Invalid API Key
column4: Validate API Gateway rejects POST /classify with an invalid API key value
column5: API Gateway deployed; usage plan active
column6: 1. Send POST /v1/classify with x-api-key: invalid-key-value\n2. Verify status code\n3. Verify rejection is logged
column7: HTTP 403 Forbidden
column14: Tests both SEC-001 and SEC-002 scenarios from security plan

column3: Classify Endpoint — End-to-End via API Gateway
column4: Validate full path from FlutterFlow-style request through API Gateway to Classifier Lambda to Bedrock and back
column5: API Gateway anp-api-dev deployed; valid API key in Secrets Manager; Bedrock available
column6:
1. Retrieve Dev API key from Secrets Manager anp/dev/api-key
2. Send POST ${API_URL}/v1/classify with x-api-key header and valid song lyric body
3. Verify HTTP 200
4. Verify response JSON contains mood_label and confidence_score
5. Verify request appears in CloudWatch access logs
column7: HTTP 200; valid mood label returned; API Gateway access log entry created within 30 seconds
column14: Primary integration path — replicates exact FlutterFlow call pattern

column3: Recommend Endpoint — Valid User with History
column4: Validate GET /recommend returns personalized playlist for a user with existing listening history
column5: anp-recommender-dev deployed; anp-user-history-dev seeded with test user records; anp-catalog-moods-dev seeded; valid Cognito JWT for test user available
column6:
1. Obtain Cognito JWT for test user with seeded listening history
2. Send GET ${API_URL}/v1/recommend?mood=Peaceful with Authorization: Bearer <JWT>
3. Verify HTTP 200
4. Verify response contains playlist array
5. Verify playlist items exclude recently played content_ids
column7: HTTP 200 with playlist array containing >= 1 item; mood_label on each item matches requested mood
column14: Core recommendation use case; validates DynamoDB GSI query on mood_label-index

column3: Recommend Endpoint — Cold Start New User
column4: Validate GET /recommend returns a playlist for a brand-new user with no listening history
column5: anp-recommender-dev deployed; anp-catalog-moods-dev seeded; valid Cognito JWT for a new test user with no history records
column6:
1. Obtain Cognito JWT for a new user not present in anp-user-history-dev
2. Send GET ${API_URL}/v1/recommend?mood=Joyful with Authorization: Bearer <JWT>
3. Verify HTTP 200
4. Verify playlist array is not empty
column7: HTTP 200 with non-empty playlist array; no 404 or 500 error
column14: Cold-start scenario from DDD Data Flow Design — must return 200 not 404

column4: Validate GET /recommend returns HTTP 401 when Authorization header is absent
column5: API Gateway JWT authorizer active on /recommend route
column6: 1. Send GET ${API_URL}/v1/recommend?mood=Peaceful with no Authorization header\n2. Verify response status code\n3. Verify rejection is logged
column7: HTTP 401 Unauthorized
column14: Maps to SEC-003 in security plan; must not reach Lambda

column4: Validate GET /recommend rejects an expired Cognito JWT
column5: An expired Cognito JWT for a valid user is available for testing
column6: 1. Send GET ${API_URL}/v1/recommend?mood=Peaceful with Authorization: Bearer <expired_JWT>\n2. Verify HTTP 401\n3. Confirm rejection logged in API Gateway access logs
column7: HTTP 401 Unauthorized; rejection logged
column14: Maps to SEC-004 in security plan

column3: Auto-Tagger Pipeline — S3 Upload Triggers DynamoDB Write
column4: Validate the Auto-Tagger Lambda processes a new S3 catalog upload and writes mood tag to DynamoDB within 60 seconds
column5: anp-autotagger-dev deployed; S3 event notification configured on anp-catalog-dev bucket; anp-catalog-moods-dev table available; Bedrock available
column6:
1. Upload a new lyric text file to s3://anp-catalog-<acct>-dev/catalog/<test-id>.txt
2. Wait up to 60 seconds
3. Query anp-catalog-moods-dev for item with partition key = <test-id>
4. Verify mood_label and mood_confidence attributes are present
column7: DynamoDB item created within 60 seconds; mood_label in vocabulary; mood_confidence >= 0.0
column14: Maps to TC-011 in implementation guide integration tests; validates event-driven pipeline

column4: Validate that Auto-Tagger writes a low_confidence flag when Bedrock returns confidence_score below 0.5
column5: anp-autotagger-dev deployed; a catalog text file crafted to produce ambiguous mood classification is available
column6:
1. Upload an ambiguous catalog text file designed to yield low Bedrock confidence
2. Wait 60 seconds
3. Query DynamoDB for the item
4. Check for low_confidence attribute
column7: DynamoDB item has low_confidence attribute set; item is excluded from recommendation queries
column14: Validates min_confidence_threshold logic described in DDD Data Governance section

column4: Validate the complete user journey: classify content via API then retrieve a personalized recommendation based on the classified mood
column5: Full Dev environment deployed; catalog seeded; test user Cognito JWT available
column6:
1. Send POST /v1/classify with valid lyric text and valid API key
2. Record returned mood_label
3. Send GET /v1/recommend?mood=<returned_mood_label> with valid Cognito JWT
4. Verify playlist reflects the mood label from step 1
5. Verify all responses are HTTP 200
column7: Both endpoints return HTTP 200; recommend playlist mood_label matches classify output; end-to-end flow completes in <= 4 seconds total
column14: Primary user journey; validates system integration across all three Lambda functions

column3: API Request Payload Validation — Oversized Text
column4: Validate that the Classifier Lambda handles a text payload exceeding 4000 characters gracefully
column5: API Gateway deployed; Classifier Lambda deployed
column6:
1. Construct a text payload with > 4000 characters
2. Send POST /v1/classify with the oversized text and valid API key
3. Verify that the Lambda truncates input to 4000 chars and returns a valid response
4. Confirm no Lambda error or timeout
column7: HTTP 200 with valid mood label; no 500 error; Lambda truncates input as per classifier.py build_prompt logic
column14: Validates truncation logic in classifier.py prompt builder; prevents context window overflow

column4: Validate GET /recommend returns HTTP 400 for a mood label not in the defined vocabulary
column5: API Gateway and Recommender Lambda deployed
column6: 1. Send GET /v1/recommend?mood=Angry with valid Cognito JWT\n2. Verify response status code\n3. Verify error message describes unsupported mood value
column7: HTTP 400 with descriptive error message
column14: Maps to TC-010 in implementation guide; validates input validation at Lambda or API Gateway level

column4: Validate that HTTP (non-HTTPS) requests to the API Gateway endpoint are rejected
column5: API Gateway deployed with HTTPS-only enforcement configured
column6: 1. Construct the API Gateway base URL using http:// instead of https://\n2. Send GET request to the HTTP variant of the endpoint\n3. Verify HTTP 403 response
column7: HTTP 403 Forbidden; TLS enforcement confirmed
column14: Maps to SEC-006 in security plan and TC-012 in implementation guide

column3: DynamoDB PITR Enabled on Both Tables
column4: Validate DynamoDB Point-in-Time Recovery is enabled on both production tables before go-live
column5: Production deployment complete; anp-catalog-moods-prod and anp-user-history-prod tables created
column6: 1. Run aws dynamodb describe-continuous-backups on anp-catalog-moods-prod\n2. Run aws dynamodb describe-continuous-backups on anp-user-history-prod\n3. Verify ContinuousBackupsStatus = ENABLED on both tables
column7: ContinuousBackupsStatus: ENABLED on both tables
column14: SOW Go-Live Readiness criterion — must be verified before Production go-live approval

column4: Validate p95 response time for both endpoints under 10 concurrent users baseline load
column5: Dev environment fully deployed; Artillery CLI installed; valid Dev API key retrieved from Secrets Manager; test Cognito JWTs available
column6:
1. Configure Artillery test: 10 virtual users, 5-minute duration, targeting POST /v1/classify and GET /v1/recommend
2. Execute load test
3. Collect p95 latency from Artillery report
4. Record results
column7: p95 latency <= 2000ms for both endpoints; zero HTTP 5xx errors; error rate = 0%
column14: Baseline measurement before moderate load test; establishes cold-start frequency

column4: Validate p95 response time for both endpoints at the SOW-defined acceptance load of 50 concurrent users
column5: Dev environment stable; baseline test NFT-001 passed; Artillery configured with 50 VUs
column6:
1. Configure Artillery test: 50 virtual users, 5-minute sustained load
2. Execute load test with JSON output
3. Parse p95 from report
4. Confirm error rate < 1%
column7: p95 latency <= 2000ms for POST /classify AND GET /recommend; error rate < 1%; zero HTTP 5xx
column14: SOW success metric and go-live criterion — must pass before Production deployment

column4: Characterize Classifier Lambda cold-start latency to determine if provisioned concurrency is required
column5: Dev Classifier Lambda deployed without provisioned concurrency; X-Ray tracing enabled
column6:
1. Allow Lambda to go cold (no invocations for 15 minutes)
2. Send 5 sequential POST /v1/classify requests
3. Extract Init Duration from CloudWatch Logs for first invocation
4. Compare warm vs cold p95 latency
column7: Cold-start @initDuration documented; if cold-start p95 > 2000ms provisioned concurrency will be enabled per DDD risk register
column14: Informs whether provisioned_concurrency should be set above 0 in configuration.csv

column3: Lambda Power Tuning — Classifier Memory Optimization
column4: Validate that the 1024 MB Classifier Lambda memory allocation is optimal for p95 latency target
column5: AWS Lambda Power Tuning tool deployed in Dev account
column6:
1. Deploy Lambda Power Tuning state machine with classifier test lyric payload
2. Test memory values: 512, 1024, 1536, 2048 MB
3. Review power tuning report for cost-vs-speed recommendation
4. Confirm 1024 MB meets p95 target at lowest cost
column7: Lambda Power Tuning report confirms 1024 MB is optimal or identifies a superior memory setting; p95 remains <= 2000ms at recommended setting
column14: DDD compute sizing note — run during Phase 3; results documented in Test Results Report

column4: Validate Auto-Tagger processes a single catalog upload within the SOW-defined 60-second SLA
column5: anp-autotagger-dev deployed; S3 event notification active; CloudWatch logging enabled
column6:
1. Record upload timestamp T0 for a single catalog file uploaded to S3
2. Poll DynamoDB for the new item every 5 seconds
3. Record timestamp T1 when item appears
4. Calculate processing time T1 - T0
column7: Processing time T1 - T0 <= 60 seconds
column14: SOW success metric — auto-tagging within 60 seconds of S3 upload

column4: Validate that all three API key failure scenarios return HTTP 403 and do not invoke Lambda
column5: API Gateway anp-api-dev deployed with usage plan; valid and invalid API keys available
column6:
1. Send POST /v1/classify with no x-api-key header — expect HTTP 403
2. Send POST /v1/classify with x-api-key: invalid-value — expect HTTP 403
3. Send POST /v1/classify with a revoked API key — expect HTTP 403
4. Verify CloudWatch Lambda invocation count did NOT increment for any rejected request
column7: HTTP 403 for all three cases; Lambda invocation count = 0 for rejected requests; all rejections logged in API GW access logs
column14: Maps to SEC-001 and SEC-002; verifies rejection occurs at API Gateway not Lambda

column3: Cognito JWT Validation — Three Failure Scenarios
column4: Validate that GET /recommend rejects missing and expired JWTs and JWTs from an unrecognized pool with HTTP 401
column5: API Gateway JWT authorizer configured with anp-users-dev Cognito User Pool; expired JWT and wrong-pool JWT available for testing
column6: 1. Send GET /v1/recommend?mood=Peaceful with no Authorization header — expect HTTP 401\n2. Send GET /v1/recommend?mood=Peaceful with Authorization: Bearer <expired_JWT> — expect HTTP 401\n3. Send GET /v1/recommend?mood=Peaceful with JWT from a different Cognito User Pool — expect HTTP 401
column7: HTTP 401 for all three cases; Recommender Lambda not invoked; all rejections logged
column14: Maps to SEC-003/SEC-004/SEC-005 in implementation guide security test matrix

column4: Validate each Lambda execution role cannot access AWS services outside its permitted scope
column5: AWS IAM Policy Simulator available; all three Lambda execution roles deployed
column6: 1. Run IAM Policy Simulator for anp-classifier-lambda-role against dynamodb:GetItem and s3:PutObject — expect implicitDeny\n2. Run for anp-recommender-lambda-role against bedrock:InvokeModel and s3:GetObject — expect implicitDeny\n3. Run for anp-autotagger-lambda-role against dynamodb:DeleteItem on non-catalog tables — expect implicitDeny
column7: All out-of-scope actions return implicitDeny; zero wildcard resource permissions detected
column14: Maps to SEC-007/SEC-008/SEC-009; DDD role definitions table must be satisfied

column3: Secrets Validation — No Plaintext Credentials in Lambda Config or Logs
column4: Validate no API keys or Firebase credentials appear in Lambda environment variables or CloudWatch logs
column5: All Lambda functions deployed; CloudWatch Logs populated with at least 30 minutes of invocation logs
column6: 1. Run aws lambda get-function-configuration for all three Lambdas and inspect Environment.Variables — confirm no raw secrets\n2. Run CloudWatch Log Insights query on /aws/lambda/anp-classifier for keywords: password secret_key client_secret\n3. Confirm Log Insights query returns zero matching events
column7: Zero plaintext secrets in Lambda environment variables; zero credential matches in CloudWatch logs
column14: Maps to SEC-010 and SEC-011; Secrets Manager secret ARNs are acceptable as env vars but not raw values

column3: Encryption at Rest — S3 and DynamoDB
column4: Validate all data at rest is encrypted using the required algorithms
column5: S3 catalog bucket and DynamoDB tables created; AWS CLI access available
column6:
1. Run aws s3api get-bucket-encryption on anp-catalog-<acct>-dev — verify SSEAlgorithm = AES256
2. Run aws dynamodb describe-table for anp-catalog-moods-dev — verify SSEStatus = ENABLED
3. Run aws dynamodb describe-table for anp-user-history-dev — verify SSEStatus = ENABLED
4. Run aws s3api head-object on a sample S3 catalog file — verify ServerSideEncryption = AES256
column7: S3 SSEAlgorithm = AES256; DynamoDB SSEStatus = ENABLED on both tables; S3 objects individually encrypted
column14: Maps to SEC-012 and SEC-013; DDD encryption at rest requirements

column4: Validate AWS CloudTrail is actively logging all API-level activity with 90-day retention
column5: CloudTrail configured per implementation guide Phase 1 procedure; S3 bucket for CloudTrail logs exists
column6: 1. Run aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name anp-audit-trail — verify IsLogging = true\n2. Verify LatestDeliveryTime is within the last 2 hours\n3. Confirm S3 log retention lifecycle policy is set to 90 days
column7: IsLogging = true; LatestDeliveryTime within 2 hours; S3 lifecycle policy = 90 days
column14: Maps to SEC-014; DDD compliance mappings CC7.2

column3: Input Validation at API Gateway — Malformed JSON Body
column4: Validate that API Gateway request model validation rejects malformed JSON before invoking any Lambda
column5: API Gateway request model validator active on POST /v1/classify
column6: 1. Send POST /v1/classify with malformed JSON body and valid API key\n2. Verify HTTP 400 is returned\n3. Verify Lambda invocation count did NOT increment
column7: HTTP 400 from API Gateway before Lambda invocation; Lambda CloudWatch invocation count = 0
column14: Maps to SEC-015 in implementation guide security test matrix; protects Lambda from parsing errors

column4: Validate DynamoDB Point-in-Time Recovery restores the catalog-moods table to a test table with data integrity intact
column5: DynamoDB PITR enabled on anp-catalog-moods-dev; table has at least 50 items seeded
column6:
1. Note current item count in anp-catalog-moods-dev
2. Initiate PITR restore to a new table anp-catalog-moods-pitr-test
3. Wait for restore to complete
4. Verify item count in restored table matches source
5. Spot-check 5 items for attribute integrity
6. Delete the test restore table
column7: Restored table item count matches source +/- 1%; all spot-checked items have correct mood_label and confidence_score; restore completes within 15 minutes
column14: SOW Go-Live Readiness criterion; DDD RPO = 24 hours; validates 35-day backup window

column4: Validate that all Lambda functions can be re-deployed from IaC within the SOW-defined 1-hour RTO
column5: CDK IaC codebase in GitHub; Dev environment fully deployed
column6:
1. Delete all three Lambda functions from Dev using AWS CLI
2. Record timestamp T0
3. Execute full CDK deploy for ANPComputeDevStack
4. Record timestamp T1 when all three Lambdas are in Active state
5. Validate each function responds to a test invocation
column7: T1 - T0 <= 30 minutes; all three Lambdas Active; test invocations return valid responses; RTO of 1 hour confirmed achievable
column14: DDD disaster recovery section — IaC re-deployment expected to complete in 15-30 minutes

column4: Validate that all configured CloudWatch alarms successfully deliver SNS email notifications when triggered
column5: CloudWatch alarms deployed; SNS topic anp-ops-alerts-dev created; email subscription confirmed by ANP technical contact
column6: 1. Manually set each of the 7 alarms to ALARM state using aws cloudwatch set-alarm-state\n2. Confirm SNS email received by ANP technical contact within 5 minutes for each alarm\n3. Reset all alarms to OK state after confirmation
column7: SNS email notification received for each of the 7 configured alarms within 5 minutes of trigger; all alarms reset to OK state successfully
column14: DDD alert definitions table — validates all 7 alarm notification paths before Production go-live

column4: Validate that failed Auto-Tagger events route to the DLQ and trigger the CloudWatch alarm and can be reprocessed manually
column5: anp-autotagger-dlq-dev SQS queue created; CloudWatch alarm anp-autotagger-dlq-depth-dev configured; a malformed test S3 object available
column6:
1. Upload a malformed S3 catalog file designed to cause Auto-Tagger Lambda to fail all 3 retries
2. Wait for DLQ message to appear
3. Confirm CloudWatch alarm enters ALARM state
4. Retrieve DLQ message and inspect S3 event payload
5. Re-upload a corrected file and confirm DynamoDB is updated
column7: DLQ receives message after 3 Lambda retries; CloudWatch alarm triggers; DLQ message contains original S3 event payload; reprocessed file writes to DynamoDB successfully
column14: DDD messaging section; validates SQS DLQ bisect-on-error and runbook reprocessing procedure

column4: Validate that the Classifier Lambda achieves >= 90% mood/emotion label accuracy against a labeled sample of faith-based content
column5: A labeled test dataset of >= 50 faith-based lyric and transcript items with known correct mood labels is prepared; Classifier Lambda deployed; valid Dev API key available
column6:
1. For each of the 50+ labeled items send POST /v1/classify with the item text
2. Record returned mood_label vs expected label
3. Calculate accuracy as correct_labels divided by total_items multiplied by 100
4. Document per-mood accuracy breakdown
column7: Accuracy >= 90% overall; per-label precision documented; items with confidence_score < 0.5 excluded from accuracy denominator
column14: SOW success metric: >= 90% classification accuracy; go-live criterion M3

column3: Classify Real Catalog Items — 20 Sample Songs and Podcasts
column4: ANP technical contact validates that POST /classify returns reasonable mood labels for 20 real faith-based catalog items
column5: Production-like Dev UAT environment; ANP technical contact has Dev API key; 20 real catalog lyric and transcript texts sourced from ANP Firebase catalog are available
column6:
1. ANP technical contact selects 20 catalog items (mix of songs and podcasts) from the Firebase export
2. Submit each item text to POST /v1/classify using the Dev API key
3. Review each returned mood_label for reasonableness against the content's spiritual character
4. Record acceptance or rejection per item
5. Calculate overall acceptance rate
column7: >= 90% of returned mood labels judged reasonable by ANP technical contact; no mood labels returned outside the defined vocabulary
column14: SOW UAT requirement — 20 real catalog items; accuracy validated by ANP stakeholder not just automated label match

column3: Personalized Playlist Relevance for 5 Test Users
column4: ANP technical contact validates that GET /recommend returns relevant playlists for 5 test users with varying listening histories
column5: anp-user-history-dev seeded with 5 test user profiles with distinct listening patterns; anp-catalog-moods-dev seeded; Cognito JWTs for test users available
column6:
1. For each of 5 test users call GET /v1/recommend with their Cognito JWT and a mood label matching their history
2. Review returned playlist items for relevance to the user's faith-listening profile
3. Confirm recently-played items are excluded from recommendations
4. Record acceptance per user
column7: Playlists judged relevant for >= 4 of 5 test users; recently played items correctly excluded; no 500 errors or empty playlists
column14: SOW UAT requirement — 5 test users with varying histories; validates recommendation personalization from business perspective

column3: Auto-Tagger Validates New Content Upload Flow
column4: ANP technical contact validates that uploading a new catalog item results in a correct mood tag in DynamoDB within 60 seconds
column5: Dev environment; ANP technical contact has S3 upload access to anp-catalog-dev bucket; DynamoDB console access available
column6:
1. ANP technical contact selects a new catalog item not previously in DynamoDB
2. Upload the lyric text file to s3://anp-catalog-<acct>-dev/catalog/<content-id>.txt
3. Wait up to 60 seconds
4. Navigate to DynamoDB console and retrieve the new item
5. Review mood_label and confidence_score
column7: DynamoDB item appears within 60 seconds; mood_label is judged reasonable by ANP technical contact; confidence_score is present
column14: SOW UAT — validates auto-tagging pipeline from client perspective; simulates post-handover catalog management workflow

column3: FlutterFlow App Can Call Production API Without Frontend Changes
column4: ANP technical contact and FlutterFlow developer confirm the Production API is callable from a FlutterFlow test build using the Production API key and Cognito token
column5: Production environment deployed; Production API key distributed to ANP FlutterFlow developer; Cognito User Pool anp-users-prod configured; FlutterFlow test build available
column6:
1. ANP FlutterFlow developer adds the Production API base URL and API key to the FlutterFlow API configuration
2. Trigger POST /classify from within the FlutterFlow app using a sample lyric
3. Trigger GET /recommend from within the FlutterFlow app using a test user Cognito JWT
4. Verify both responses are received correctly in the app
5. Confirm no FlutterFlow frontend code changes were required
column7: Both endpoints return HTTP 200 from the FlutterFlow app; response data is correctly received; zero frontend code changes were made
column14: SOW success metric: REST API callable from FlutterFlow with zero frontend changes; go-live criterion M6

column4: ANP Streaming confirms the six defined mood labels are appropriate for faith-based Christian music and podcast content
column5: A list of the six mood labels (Joyful/Reflective/Peaceful/Uplifting/Worshipful/Hopeful) and their definitions is provided to ANP stakeholders
column6:
1. Present the six mood labels and their definitions to Lilly Goyah and ANP technical contact
2. Ask each stakeholder to evaluate whether the vocabulary captures the spiritual character of ANP's catalog
3. Identify any labels that should be added, removed, or renamed
4. Document accepted vocabulary in the project sign-off record
column7: Lilly Goyah and ANP technical contact accept all six mood labels as appropriate for the faith-based catalog; any vocabulary changes are documented as a potential scope change
column14: Vocabulary is used across all three Lambda functions and must be agreed before go-live; changes post-go-live require a Lambda redeploy

column4: ANP technical contact confirms the ANP-Operations CloudWatch dashboard provides actionable visibility into API health
column5: CloudWatch ANP-Operations dashboard deployed; Dev environment generating API traffic
column6:
1. ANP technical contact logs into AWS Console with their named IAM account
2. Navigate to the ANP-Operations CloudWatch dashboard
3. Identify current Lambda error rates and p95 latency and API invocation counts
4. Confirm each metric panel has a clear title and is understandable without additional guidance
5. Locate the DLQ depth panel and confirm it reads 0
column7: ANP technical contact can navigate to and interpret the dashboard independently; all panels are clearly labeled; DLQ depth = 0; no training required beyond the TRN-002 module
column14: Validates operational handover readiness; maps to TRN-002 training module and runbook section

column3: Go-Live Readiness Sign-Off — All Criteria Met
column4: Lilly Goyah and ANP technical contact jointly confirm all SOW go-live readiness criteria have been satisfied and approve Production deployment
column5: All functional tests (>= 95% pass); all security tests (100% pass); p95 latency <= 2000ms at 50 concurrent users; Bedrock accuracy >= 90%; DynamoDB PITR enabled; CloudWatch alarms confirmed; API documentation reviewed
column6:
1. QA Lead presents the Test Results Report (SOW Deliverable #15) to Lilly Goyah and ANP technical contact
2. Walkthrough each go-live criterion from the SOW checklist
3. Lilly Goyah confirms executive approval for Production go-live
4. ANP technical contact signs off UAT completion
5. Jonas Bull records the go-live approval confirmation in the Project Closeout Report
column7: All SOW go-live criteria confirmed as PASS; Lilly Goyah provides written executive go-live approval; ANP technical contact signs UAT completion form
column14: SOW Go-Live Readiness section — all criteria must be satisfied; this sign-off gates the Production deployment milestone M6

column4: ANP technical contact confirms the API Reference Documentation is complete and sufficient to integrate the REST API from the FlutterFlow app
column5: API Reference Documentation (SOW Deliverable #17) delivered to ANP technical contact for 3-business-day review
column6:
1. ANP technical contact reviews the API reference for both endpoints
2. Verify documentation includes endpoint URLs, authentication instructions, request/response schemas, error codes, and FlutterFlow example calls
3. Attempt to construct a POST /classify request using only the documentation (no external guidance)
4. Document any gaps or ambiguities found
column7: ANP technical contact accepts the document; is able to construct a valid API request using only the reference documentation; any identified gaps are resolved before acceptance
column14: SOW success metric: API documentation accepted by ANP technical contact by Week 6

column4: ANP technical contact confirms the Operational Runbook is sufficient for independent steady-state operations after hypercare ends
column5: Operational Runbook (SOW Deliverable #18) delivered; ANP technical contact has participated in TRN-004 knowledge transfer session
column6:
1. ANP technical contact reviews the runbook independently
2. Locate and walkthrough the procedure for the Classifier Lambda error rate alarm
3. Locate the API key rotation procedure
4. Locate the DynamoDB PITR restore procedure
5. Identify any missing procedures or unclear steps
column7: ANP technical contact accepts the runbook; can locate all three procedures independently without assistance; any gaps are resolved within the 3-business-day review window
column14: SOW success metric: runbook accepted by ANP technical contact by Week 6; prerequisite for full operational handover

column3: Production Smoke Test — 5 Representative API Calls
column4: Confirm the Production API is live and functional immediately following Production deployment by executing 5 representative API calls
column5: Production deployment complete; Production API key available; Cognito JWT for a test user available
column6:
1. Send POST /v1/classify to Production API Gateway URL with a faith-based lyric and Production API key
2. Verify HTTP 200 and valid mood_label returned
3. Send GET /v1/recommend to Production API with valid Cognito JWT and mood=Peaceful
4. Verify HTTP 200 and non-empty playlist returned
5. Confirm both requests appear in CloudWatch Production access logs within 60 seconds
6. Confirm Production alarms remain in OK state after smoke tests
column7: All 5 smoke test calls return HTTP 200; valid mood labels and playlist data returned; CloudWatch logs show Production traffic; alarms remain OK; go-live confirmed by Jonas Bull
column14: SOW cutover plan step 2 — 10 representative calls required; this UAT covers the minimum smoke tests; Jonas Bull confirms go-live to Lilly Goyah after pass
